notes: Seventeenth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school prepares students to achieve academic excellence through the Core Knowledge Sequence.
Official materials describe the school as a Challenge Foundation Academy.
The school says students live by an Honor Code built around consideration, integrity, positive spirit, and value of the individual.
Official pages highlight an excellent music and art program along with outdoor education.
Its board page says regular board meetings are usually held on the third Wednesday of each month.
Official pages list Ted Duncan as school director.
Its mission says the school prepares students for a lifetime of learning by developing their natural passion for discovery and problem-solving.
Official materials say the school opened in fall 2019.
The school says it teaches values of honesty, consideration, and responsibility while cultivating informed and engaged citizens.
A January 2026 announcement says the school was awarded a 10-year charter renewal by the North Carolina Charter Schools Review Board.
Its board page lists Ryan Hunt as board chair.
Official materials highlight an engaging STEM program for grades 4-8 and a growing athletic program.
Its public-facing tagline is "Inspiring excellence. Nurturing innovation."
Its mission says the school fosters academic and artistic excellence while enriching cultural literacy.
Official materials say the school has roots in North Carolina's original wave of charter schools and traces its tradition of excellence back to 1997.
The school says its K-12 program is powered by STREAM: Science, Technology, Research, Engineering, Arts, and Math.
Its board page says regular board meetings are typically held on the fourth Tuesday at 5:30 p.m.
Official pages identify Lindsey Sealy as executive director.
Its public-facing motto is "Science, Wisdom, Peace."
Its mission says the school prepares students to become competent, responsible, and successful individuals in a globalized and technology-based society.
Official materials say the school was established in 2008 by public charter school advocates.
The school says it focuses on STEAM and emphasizes a safe, nurturing, and culturally responsive learning environment.
TMSA governance pages list Victor Ofori-Boadu as board president.
Official principal pages list Fatih Kandil as principal.
Its public-facing motto is "Science, Wisdom, Peace."
Official materials say the school was established in 2012 in response to demand created by the first TMSA campus.
The school says it is a Cognia-accredited Leader in Me school dedicated to STEAM excellence.
Its public materials say it has consistently earned the highest level school performance designation since North Carolina began issuing those grades in 2013-2014.
TMSA governance pages list Victor Ofori-Boadu as board president.
Official campus pages list Benjamin Cobanli as Cary principal and Serdar Yuksekkaya as Apex principal.
